terms.definition: Supervised model that recursively partitions feature space with binary, axis-aligned splits; leaves output a class majority (classification) or mean target (regression). Canonical home for tree structure and prediction paths.

Classification And Regression Trees — the greedy top-down algorithm that builds binary trees by minimizing impurity (Gini/entropy for classification, variance for regression). Canonical definition in this topic.

Classification impurity G = 1 − Σ_k p_k² where p_k is the fraction of class k in a node. Zero for pure nodes. Default criterion in scikit-learn DecisionTreeClassifier. Canonical definition in this topic.

Classification impurity H = −Σ_k p_k log₂ p_k — uncertainty in bits before a split. Used as an alternative to Gini in CART; splits maximize information gain = H(parent) − weighted H(children). Canonical tree-impurity definition in this topic.
Reduction in impurity from a split: parent impurity minus weighted sum of child impurities. CART selects the split with maximum gain.
A rule of the form feature j ≤ threshold t that partitions node samples into left and right children. CART searches all candidate (j, t) pairs greedily at each node.
Terminal node with no further splits. Stores the prediction: majority class (classifier) or mean y (regressor) of training samples reaching that leaf.
A threshold test on a single feature at a time (hyperplane parallel to feature axes). CART does not learn oblique multi-feature splits in standard form.
